query_scores.answer: Hi, we're Falcon Ridge Logistics. Account ID FR-00421. We just went live on your platform last week. | Welcome, Falcon Ridge Logistics. Account FR-00421 is noted. How can I help? | We have 80 seats on the Enterprise plan. Our admin is Kim Park at k.park@falconridge.com. | Saved: 80 Enterprise seats, admin Kim Park (k.park@falconridge.com). | We're getting a 403 error when trying to access the Reporting module. Our SSO is set up through Okta. | Known issue: Okta SSO + Reporting module 403 error. Workaround: re-authorize the Okta app connection in Settings > Integrations, then log out and back in. Does that resolve it? | Yes, that fixed it. Thanks. | Great. Ticket closed. Workaround documented on your account. | Kim here from Falcon Ridge again. We're trying to export our shipment data to CSV and the export always fails silently after about 30 seconds. | Hi Kim. Falcon Ridge account FR-00421. query_scores.answer: I'm 34, female, 5'6", 168 lbs. Want to lose about 20 lbs and build some muscle. | Got it — 34F, 5'6", 168 lbs, goal: lose 20 lbs and build muscle. What's your current activity level? | Pretty sedentary. I sit at a desk all day. I used to run in college but haven't done it consistently in years. | Starting point: sedentary, desk job, running background. We'll build back gradually. | I have mild asthma, well-controlled with an inhaler. It flares up in cold, dry air. | Noted: mild asthma, flares in cold/dry conditions. Keep inhaler accessible for outdoor winter workouts. | I'm vegetarian. No meat, no fish. I eat eggs and dairy. | Diet: lacto-ovo vegetarian. Plenty of protein options — eggs, Greek yogurt, legumes, cottage cheese. | It's been 6 weeks. Lost 4 lbs, feel stronger. Running 3x/week now, 20-25 mins. | Great progress — 4 lbs down, running 3x/week. Current weight: 164 lbs. | I was diagnosed with iron-deficiency anemia. My doctor wants me to focus on iron-rich foods. | Iron deficiency noted. Top vegetarian iron sources: lentils, spinach, tofu, fortified cereals, pumpkin seeds. Combine with vitamin C for better absorption. Avoid coffee/tea with meals. | I started taking iron supplements in the mornings. | Iron supplement (morning) logged. Take with OJ or a vitamin C source, and avoid coffee for at least 1 hour after. | I twisted my left ankle pretty badly last week. Doctor says no running for 4-6 weeks. | Left ankle sprain — running on hold for 4-6 weeks. Switching to low-impact: swimming, cycling, upper body strength. | I don't have pool access. Can I do the elliptical? | Elliptical is fine — minimal ankle impact. Also: upper body weights, seated yoga. Avoid high-impact until cleared. | The anemia is worse actually — my ferritin came back at 8. Doctor doubled my supplement dose. | Ferritin 8 is quite low. Supplement dose doubled (now 2x morning dose). Prioritize iron-rich meals every day. | Ankle is healed. Cleared to run again as of Monday. | Running cleared. We'll restart gradually — don't jump back to 25-min runs immediately. | My weight is at 161 now. Down 7 lbs total. I want to shift the goal — less focused on weight, more on running a 5K. | Goal updated: 5K race goal, weight loss secondary. Current weight 161 lbs. Let's build a training plan. | I went vegan last month. Cutting out eggs and dairy too. | Diet updated to fully vegan. This increases protein challenge — need deliberate planning: legumes, tofu, tempeh, edamame, nutritional yeast. Iron absorption remains critical. | I signed up for a 5K on October 12. About 8 weeks out. | Race date set: October 12. 8 weeks to go — solid timeline for a goal finish. | My ferritin is at 22 now. Doctor reduced supplement back to one dose in the morning. | Ferritin improved to 22. Back to single morning iron supplement dose. | I've been getting shin splints on longer runs. Right leg mostly. | Shin splints (right leg) noted. Reduce mileage 20%, increase rest days, check shoe cushioning. Avoid running consecutive days until resolved. | I finished the 5K! Time was 34:22. | Congratulations — 5K done at 34:22. That's a great first race. | Weight is 158 lbs now. I feel good where I am. New goal is just to maintain and run a 10K next spring. | Goal updated: maintain 158 lbs, train for 10K next spring. | Shin splints are gone. I think it was my old shoes. | Shin splints resolved — attributed to footwear. Note: replace running shoes every 300-500 miles to prevent recurrence.
query_scores.expected_answer: 158 lbs. Goal: maintain current weight and train for a 10K in the spring.

query_scores.answer: New opportunity: Hartwell Manufacturing, mid-market account. Potential deal size $120K ARR. They're evaluating our platform for supply chain analytics. | Opportunity created: Hartwell Manufacturing, $120K ARR, supply chain analytics use case. | Champion is Nadine Foster, Director of Operations. She pushed for the evaluation. Economic buyer is CFO Bill Yamamoto. | Champion: Nadine Foster (Dir. Operations). Economic buyer: Bill Yamamoto (CFO). | Main competitor is Synapse Analytics. They've been the incumbent for 2 years. Hartwell likes them but is frustrated with slow support response times. | Competitor: Synapse Analytics (2-year incumbent). Pain point: slow support. Our differentiation opportunity. | Discovery call done. Their core pain is inventory forecasting — they're losing $800K/year to overstock and they know it. | Core pain: inventory forecasting. Quantified loss: $800K/year to overstock. | They also have a new stakeholder — VP IT, Raj Patel. He's a blocker, not a champion. Worried about integration complexity. | New stakeholder: Raj Patel (VP IT). Blocker — concerned about integration complexity. | Decision timeline is Q3 end. Budget is $150K approved but they won't share it — I found out through Nadine off the record. | Budget: $150K (approved, found via Nadine, treat as confidential). Decision: Q3 end. Deal size headroom exists above $120K. | POC kicked off. Raj's team is testing the integration. Early feedback is good but they flagged an issue with our SAP connector — it doesn't support SAP S/4HANA out of the box. | POC blocker: SAP S/4HANA connector not supported out of the box. Raj Patel's team flagged. | Our solutions engineer told Raj we can build the S/4HANA connector as part of a premium implementation package for $15K. | Proposed resolution: S/4HANA connector via premium implementation package at $15K add-on. | Bill Yamamoto left the company. New CFO is Sandra Ng, starting next Monday. | Economic buyer updated: Bill Yamamoto departed. New CFO: Sandra Ng, starting Monday. Re-qualification needed. | Met Sandra Ng. She's budget-conscious — wants to start with a pilot at $80K to prove ROI before committing to the full $150K. | Sandra Ng position: pilot-first at $80K. Full $150K deal requires ROI proof. Deal structure may change. | Synapse came back with a $90K counter-offer to retain the account. Hartwell told Nadine. | Competitive update: Synapse offered $90K retention deal. They're fighting to keep the account. | We submitted our proposal: $105K base + $15K S/4HANA connector = $120K total. Same as original but with the implementation bundled. | Proposal submitted: $105K base + $15K S/4HANA connector = $120K total. | Sandra came back and said $120K is too much for a first-year deal. She wants $100K all-in and will commit to expansion in year 2. | Counter-offer: $100K all-in (year 1), verbal commitment to expansion year 2. | We agreed to $100K year 1. S/4HANA connector included. Year 2 target is $150K. | Deal closed: $100K year 1 (S/4HANA connector included), $150K year 2 target. | Raj Patel ended up being a strong supporter once the SAP issue was resolved. Nadine and Raj both signed off. | Blocker converted: Raj Patel now a supporter. Signed off with Nadine Foster.
query_scores.expected_answer: $100K for year 1 (S/4HANA connector included). Year 2 target: $150K.

scenario_results.scenario_name: AI Tutor — High School Student Over a Semester

query_scores.answer: I'm Marcus, 10th grade. I need help with Algebra 2 and Chemistry. I'm good at reading and English but math kills me. | Got it, Marcus — 10th grade, strong in English/reading, struggling with math. Algebra 2 and Chemistry are the focus. | My teacher is Mr. Harrison for Algebra 2. We're starting polynomial functions next week. | Algebra 2 with Mr. Harrison, polynomial functions coming up. | In Chemistry I got a 54% on the first exam. query_scores.answer: I'm Priya, PhD student in computational biology at UMich, third year. My advisor is Dr. Kwon. | Context: Priya, 3rd-year CompBio PhD, University of Michigan, advisor Dr. Kwon. | My thesis is on predicting protein-protein interaction networks from single-cell RNA-seq data using graph neural networks. | Thesis: PPI network prediction from scRNA-seq using GNNs. Frontier intersection of three fields. | I'm using PyTorch Geometric and Scanpy as my main tools. Running experiments on a 40-GPU cluster at UMich. | Tech stack: PyTorch Geometric, Scanpy. Compute: UMich 40-GPU cluster. | My main dataset is the Human Cell Atlas — about 1.2M cells across 33 tissues. | Primary dataset: Human Cell Atlas, 1.2M cells, 33 tissues. | Found a key paper: Yao et al. 2023 in Nature Methods — they use a graph autoencoder for PPI prediction but only on bulk RNA-seq. My novelty is the single-cell resolution. | Key related work: Yao et al. 2023 (Nature Methods), graph autoencoder for PPI from bulk RNA-seq. Priya's novelty: single-cell resolution. | I initially planned to use a vanilla GCN but Dr. Kwon suggested Graph Transformers might capture long-range dependencies better. I'm switching. | Architecture decision: vanilla GCN dropped, switching to Graph Transformers per Dr. Kwon's advice for better long-range dependency capture. | Dr. Kwon also wants me to benchmark against STRING database predictions. Adding that to the eval plan. | Eval plan updated: add STRING database benchmark as a comparison baseline. | First experiments done. Graph Transformer is overfitting badly — validation AUC is 0.61 while training AUC hits 0.97. Not good. | Overfitting issue: Graph Transformer training AUC 0.97, validation AUC 0.61. Large gap — classic overfit. | Dr. Kwon reviewed and said the model complexity is too high for the number of labeled PPI edges we have. He wants me to try a simpler GraphSAGE approach with dropout. | Architecture change: Graph Transformer → GraphSAGE with dropout. Reason: too complex for labeled data volume. | Also found a problem in my preprocessing — I was using raw counts instead of normalized log1p counts. Fixed. This changes all prior results. | CRITICAL bug: raw counts used instead of log1p normalized. Fixed. All prior experiments are invalid — need rerun. | After the normalization fix and switching to GraphSAGE: validation AUC is 0.84. Much better. | Results improved significantly: validation AUC 0.84 with GraphSAGE + correct normalization. | We also outperform STRING baseline by 7 points on the held-out tissue panel. Dr. Kwon is happy. | Benchmark: +7% AUC over STRING baseline. Dr. Kwon satisfied with progress. | My thesis chapter 2 draft is done. Dr. Kwon wants me to add a section on computational complexity — he thinks reviewers will ask about scalability. | Chapter 2 draft complete. Action: add computational complexity/scalability section per Dr. Kwon. | Got access to a new dataset — Allen Brain Cell Atlas, 3.4M cells across the nervous system. This is huge. | New dataset: Allen Brain Cell Atlas, 3.4M cells, nervous system. Nearly 3x the primary dataset size. | Dr. Kwon wants to expand the thesis to include a neural-tissue-specific PPI analysis. This adds a third thesis chapter. | Thesis scope expanded: new Chapter 3 on neural-tissue PPI. Thesis now three chapters instead of two. | I switched from PyTorch Geometric to DGL — better scaling for the Allen data. Scanpy stays. | Tech stack update: PyTorch Geometric → DGL (better scaling). Scanpy unchanged. | Submitting a paper to NeurIPS workshop on computational biology. Title: 'Single-cell GNN for PPI Network Inference at Tissue Resolution.' | Paper submission: NeurIPS CompBio workshop. Title: 'Single-cell GNN for PPI Network Inference at Tissue Resolution.' | Dr. Kwon set the thesis defense date for May of next year. That's about 11 months away. | Defense date: May next year. ~11 months out. | The normalization bug I fixed earlier — I'm documenting it as a key methods contribution. The correct log1p treatment turns out to matter a lot for downstream GNN stability. | Methods contribution: log1p normalization importance for GNN stability documented as a finding. What started as a bug became a validated insight.
query_scores.expected_answer: GraphSAGE with dropout. Started with GCN (original plan), switched to Graph Transformer (Dr. Kwon's suggestion), then switched again to GraphSAGE due to overfitting.

I'm switching. | Architecture decision: vanilla GCN dropped, switching to Graph Transformers per Dr. Kwon's advice for better long-range dependency capture. | Dr. Kwon also wants me to benchmark against STRING database predictions. Adding that to the eval plan. | Eval plan updated: add STRING database benchmark as a comparison baseline. | First experiments done. Graph Transformer is overfitting badly — validation AUC is 0.61 while training AUC hits 0.97. Not good. | Overfitting issue: Graph Transformer training AUC 0.97, validation AUC 0.61. Large gap — classic overfit. | Dr. Kwon reviewed and said the model complexity is too high for the number of labeled PPI edges we have. He wants me to try a simpler GraphSAGE approach with dropout. | Architecture change: Graph Transformer → GraphSAGE with dropout. Reason: too complex for labeled data volume. | Also found a problem in my preprocessing — I was using raw counts instead of normalized log1p counts. Fixed. This changes all prior results. | CRITICAL bug: raw counts used instead of log1p normalized. Fixed. All prior experiments are invalid — need rerun. | After the normalization fix and switching to GraphSAGE: validation AUC is 0.84. Much better. | Results improved significantly: validation AUC 0.84 with GraphSAGE + correct normalization. | We also outperform STRING baseline by 7 points on the held-out tissue panel. Dr. Kwon is happy. | Benchmark: +7% AUC over STRING baseline. Dr. Kwon satisfied with progress. | My thesis chapter 2 draft is done. Dr. Kwon wants me to add a section on computational complexity — he thinks reviewers will ask about scalability. | Chapter 2 draft complete. Action: add computational complexity/scalability section per Dr. Kwon. | Got access to a new dataset — Allen Brain Cell Atlas, 3.4M cells across the nervous system. This is huge. | New dataset: Allen Brain Cell Atlas, 3.4M cells, nervous system. Nearly 3x the primary dataset size. | Dr. Kwon wants to expand the thesis to include a neural-tissue-specific PPI analysis. This adds a third thesis chapter. | Thesis scope expanded: new Chapter 3 on neural-tissue PPI. Thesis now three chapters instead of two. | I switched from PyTorch Geometric to DGL — better scaling for the Allen data. Scanpy stays. | Tech stack update: PyTorch Geometric → DGL (better scaling). Scanpy unchanged. | Submitting a paper to NeurIPS workshop on computational biology. Title: 'Single-cell GNN for PPI Network Inference at Tissue Resolution.' | Paper submission: NeurIPS CompBio workshop. Title: 'Single-cell GNN for PPI Network Inference at Tissue Resolution.' | Dr. Kwon set the thesis defense date for May of next year. That's about 11 months away. | Defense date: May next year. ~11 months out. | The normalization bug I fixed earlier — I'm documenting it as a key methods contribution. The correct log1p treatment turns out to matter a lot for downstream GNN stability. | Methods contribution: log1p normalization importance for GNN stability documented as a finding. What started as a bug became a validated insight.

query_scores.expected_answer: 1) Started with vanilla GCN (original plan). 2) Switched to Graph Transformer (Dr. Kwon's suggestion for long-range dependencies). 3) Graph Transformer overfit severely (train AUC 0.97, val 0.61). 4) Switched to GraphSAGE with dropout (Dr. Kwon: too complex for labeled data). 5) Also fixed log1p normalization bug — reran all experiments. 6) GraphSAGE achieved validation AUC 0.84.
